description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a large, convex glass screen encased in a rectangular wooden cabinet. The front panel features a prominent display area, a vertical speaker grille, and a set of physical control knobs and buttons, including a large rotary dial and several push buttons. The sides and top of the cabinet are finished in a warm, medium-brown wood veneer, with ventilation grilles visible on both sides and the top. The back panel is made of a dark brown molded material with additional ventilation slots and access points for maintenance. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of mid-20th-century televisions, and the object sits on four small feet. This television would likely be found in a living room, garage, or as discarded urban furniture.
description: A vintage, boxy wooden television set with a convex glass screen, physical control knobs, and side ventilation grilles.
description_view_0: The front view shows the large convex screen, speaker grille, and a cluster of control knobs and buttons on the right.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the wood veneer finish and a rectangular ventilation grille.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a dark brown molded back panel with extensive ventilation slots and access points.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wood finish and another ventilation grille.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, brown wood veneer bezel with a matte finish and rectangular shape
attribute_control_button_layout: cluster of rotary dial and push buttons on the lower right front panel

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
